etymology.description: semantic 肉(肉) (ròu, "flesh; body") + phonetic 府(府) (fǔ) -> ferment; spoil; decay
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 肉(肉) (ròu, "flesh; body") suggests organic matter, which is prone to fermentation or decay. The phonetic component 府(府) (fǔ) provides the sound aspect. Together, they represent the concept of 'fermenting' or 'decaying' as it relates to organic material.
etymology.simplification: 府(府) (fǔ), originally a separate character, retains its form, so 腐 remains unchanged in the simplified script.

examples.chinese: 这块肉已经变质腐臭(這塊肉已經變質腐臭)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè kuài ròu yǐjīng biànzhì fǔchòu.
examples.english: This piece of meat has already become rotten and smelly.

examples.chinese: 由于长时间存放(由於長時間存放)，蔬菜开始腐烂(蔬菜開始腐爛)。
examples.pinyin: Yóuyú cháng shíjiān cúnfàng, shūcài kāishǐ fǔlàn.
examples.english: Due to long-term storage, the vegetables began to decay.

examples.chinese: 他不小心吃了腐坏的食物(他不小心吃了腐壞的食物)，感到不适(感到不適)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bù xiǎoxīn chīle fǔhuài de shíwù, gǎndào bùshì.
examples.english: He accidentally ate spoiled food and felt unwell.
